commit.message:
fix: remove duplicate note notifications and prevent future duplicates

- Add migration to clean existing duplicate notifications (keeps oldest per user+note)
- Add duplicate check in immediate note publish (notes.py)
- Add duplicate check in scheduled note publisher (main.py)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

GAP-004: Section 5 backfill integration (backfillDecouplingWithGAP)

* GAP-001: Add core Grade Adjusted Pace calculation library

Implements lib/gap.ts — a pure TypeScript GAP library using the Minetti
et al. (2002) 5th-degree polynomial metabolic cost curve. Provides
minettiCost, clampGrade, lapGrade, lapGapPace, and computeGAP with
distance-weighted activity-level averages. Companion test file covers 42
cases including Minetti curve shape, grade clamping, null-safety guards,
and a 10-lap regression fixture. All tests pass via npm test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* GAP-002: SQL migration for activity_gap and lap_gap tables

Creates two tables for Section 6 Grade Adjusted Pace analytics:
- activity_gap: one row per activity storing the activity-level GAP summary
- lap_gap: one row per (activity, lap) storing per-lap grade and GAP pace

Both tables follow the established patterns: RLS disabled, GRANT to anon/authenticated,
soft TEXT reference to garmin_activities (no FK). Extends athlete_notifications
CHECK constraint to include 'gap_anomaly' using the drop-and-recreate idempotency pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* GAP-003: Add Supabase I/O pipeline for Grade Adjusted Pace

Creates lib/gapRecalc.ts with computeGAPForActivity(), computeGAPBatch(),
and triggerDecouplingBackfill(). Reads laps from garmin_activity_laps,
upserts per-lap results to lap_gap and activity-level summary to
activity_gap, then delegates Section 5 re-computation to
backfillDecouplingWithGAP(). Sequential batch processing; all errors
returned as { ok: false, error } — no exceptions propagate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* GAP-004: Implement backfillDecouplingWithGAP() — replace stub with full pipeline

Replaces the NotImplementedError stub in lib/decouplingRecalc.ts with the
complete backfill implementation. Queries activity_decoupling for awaiting_gap
rows, applies GAP-adjusted distances when total_ascent_m > 100m and
gap_applied is true (using lap_gap.gap_pace_sec_per_km), re-runs
computeDecoupling(), and upserts results with gap_used and awaiting_gap=false.
Adds GAP_ASCENT_THRESHOLD_M = 100 constant alongside SINGLE_ATHLETE_ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove old image files from root (already moved to images/)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add comprehensive CONTRIBUTING.md

Covers dev setup, attack JSON schema, detection method guidelines,
refusal override explanation, code rules, data flow, PR process,
and what gets rejected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

TASK-002: Scaffold full project — Next.js + Prisma + PostgreSQL + CI (#1)

- Tech stack: Next.js 14 (App Router, TypeScript), PostgreSQL 16 + Prisma ORM,
  Anthropic SDK, Tailwind CSS, Jest, GitHub Actions CI
- Folder structure: layered architecture (app / services / pipeline / domain / lib)
  per ADR-001; cross-layer import rules documented in ADR-009
- Prisma schema: all 10 models from V1-SPEC section 7 (cases, documents,
  extracted_fields, missing_items, local_plans, local_plan_rules,
  rule_evaluations, generated_texts, analysis_runs, audit_events)
- Prompt stubs: 5 files in /prompts/ with purpose, input/output schema, model
- CI: GitHub Actions — lint, format check, typecheck, tests, build on every PR
- Local dev: docker-compose.yml (PostgreSQL) + npm run dev;
  single npm run setup bootstraps everything
- ARCHITECTURE.md: added ADR-008 (stack), ADR-009 (folder structure),
  ADR-010 (local file storage)

Co-authored-by: Jonas El-Kabouli <hello@hooch.dk>

commit.message:
v1.09: 활동 페이지 Supabase 클라이언트 교체 — ISR 연동 버그 수정

createClient(server.ts)는 cookies()에 의존하는 SSR 클라이언트라
ISR 백그라운드 렌더링 시 cookie 컨텍스트가 없어 throw → try/catch가
조용히 잡아 빈 배열 반환 → 항상 빈 화면.

createClientForBuild(build.ts)로 교체: 순수 anon key, cookie 불필요.
공개 읽기 전용 페이지에 올바른 클라이언트. Supabase error 필드 명시
확인 및 콘솔 로깅 추가.

commit.message:
fix(db): defer DATABASE_URL error to query time to prevent Lambda crash (#56)

When DATABASE_URL is missing at runtime, createPrismaClient() throws during
module initialization, crashing the Lambda before any API route try/catch can
intercept it. Wrap the module-level export in a try/catch so initialization
failure falls back to a PrismaClient that throws at query time instead, where
existing error handlers in API routes can catch and return proper 500 responses.

Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden Pine Script output: stricter system prompt + robust extraction

System prompt now has explicit ABSOLUTE RULES for output format —
first line must be ```pinescript, first code line must be //@version=6,

raw //@version responses, and //@version buried in prose.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db): lazily initialize Prisma client to avoid cold-start crash (#55)

When DATABASE_URL is absent at serverless cold start, the eager module-level
initialization threw synchronously before any route handler try/catch could
intercept it, crashing the Lambda and surfacing as an unhandled Sentry error
at /dashboard. Switching to lazy initialization via a Proxy defers client
creation to the first actual DB query, allowing API routes to catch and handle
the error gracefully.

Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Centralize error handling with asyncHandler + typed errors

Replace 151 manual try/catch blocks across 37 route files with
asyncHandler middleware that forwards errors to the central error
handler. Routes now throw typed ApplicationError subclasses
(NotFoundError, ValidationError, ConflictError, etc.) instead of
manually setting status codes and JSON responses.

- Add asyncHandler middleware to wrap async route handlers
- Fix errorHandler to return 400 for ZodError (was defaulting to 500)
- Update all 9 route test files with error handler middleware
- Net reduction of ~600 lines of boilerplate error handling code

commit.message:
Fix canvas sizing and controls truncation

- Set small figsize on all figures (corrmat 3x3, tdist 4x2, ts 4x3):
  matplotlib's default figsize (6.4x4.8) gave canvases a large sizeHint
  that competed for space and caused the tiny-thumbnail problem. Small
  figsize lets QSizePolicy.Expanding actually grow them to fill the panel.
- Split cramped controls row into 3 rows: file+load / params+run / save.
  Eliminates text truncation on smaller/HiDPI screens.
- Lower minimum window size from 1200x820 to 800x600.

feat: add Knowledge Flywheel to goal lifecycle skills

Skills now read from and write to Kanbantic Toolkit/Library,
creating a self-maintaining knowledge base that grows with each goal:

- goal-design: queries Pattern, Gotcha, Rule, Library Architecture docs
- goal-planning: loads knowledge before exploration (targeted only),
  stores new patterns after planning, records Knowledge Trace
- goal-executing: loads knowledge instead of Explore agents,
  corrects/adds patterns after implementation, records Knowledge Trace
- implementer-prompt: passes project patterns to subagents

Knowledge Trace discussion entries provide bidirectional traceability
between goals and Toolkit/Library items.
